agent_output: {"integration_assessment":{"component_interactions":[{"from":"inventory-sync-service","to":"external-inventory-api","status":"risk","issue":"No timeout configured on outbound HTTP call. A hung upstream connection will block the sync indefinitely, potentially causing goroutine/thread leak and memory pressure over successive 5-minute intervals."},{"from":"inventory-sync-service","to":"local-database","status":"risk","issue":"If the external API returns partial or malformed data due to a degraded upstream, there is no validation gate before writing to the local DB. Silent corruption of stock counts could propagate to order fulfillment."},{"from":"cron-scheduler","to":"inventory-sync-service","status":"risk","issue":"If a sync run hangs (no timeout), the next 5-minute tick may spawn an overlapping run. No mutex or leader-election mechanism described to prevent concurrent syncs writing conflicting stock counts."}],"contract_compliance":"Unknown — no schema validation or contract tests described between the service and the external inventory API. A breaking upstream change would surface only as a runtime error.","data_flow_integrity":"At risk. Stock counts flow from external API → service → local DB with no intermediate validation, checksumming, or idempotency guarantees. A partial sync could leave the database in an inconsistent state."},"observability_review":{"logging":{"coverage":"gaps","gaps":["Only two log lines exist (success and failure). No structured logging — using string concatenation instead of JSON with fields.","Missing: sync duration, number of products synced, number of products skipped/failed, upstream response status code, upstream latency.","Missing: correlation/request IDs for tracing a single sync run end-to-end.","Error log captures err.message only — no stack trace, no upstream HTTP status, no response body snippet for diagnosis.","No log level differentiation (info vs warn vs error). A silent failure and a total outage look the same in the log stream."]},"metrics":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No sync_duration_seconds histogram — cannot alert on degrading upstream performance.","No sync_items_total counter — cannot detect partial syncs or data drift.","No sync_errors_total counter — cannot compute error rate or trigger PagerDuty.","No inventory_api_request_duration_seconds — no visibility into upstream latency.","No active_syncs gauge — cannot detect overlapping runs."]},"tracing":{"coverage":"missing","gaps":["No distributed tracing spans. A sync run touching external API + DB writes is invisible to any tracing backend (Jaeger, Datadog, etc.).","Cannot correlate a bad stock count in the DB back to the specific sync run that wrote it."]},"debugging_capability":"Severely limited. An on-call engineer at 3am would see only 'sync failed: connection refused' with no context on which products were affected, how long the outage lasted, or whether the database is now stale. There is no way to determine the last successful sync time without grepping raw logs."},"resilience_assessment":{"failure_modes":[{"scenario":"External inventory API is down","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add retry with exponential backoff (3 attempts, 1s/2s/4s base). After exhausting retries, emit a sync_failures_total metric and log at ERROR level with full context."},{"scenario":"External inventory API responds slowly or hangs","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a 30-second timeout on the outbound HTTP call. Kill and log the attempt so the next scheduled run starts clean."},{"scenario":"External inventory API returns 200 but with malformed/partial data","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Validate response schema before writing to DB. If item count drops >50% vs last known good sync, flag as anomalous and skip the write (stale-but-safe strategy)."},{"scenario":"Database write fails mid-sync (partial commit)","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Wrap the DB write in a transaction. Either all stock counts update atomically or none do. Log the rollback."},{"scenario":"Overlapping sync runs due to previous run exceeding 5-minute window","handling":"unhandled","recommendation":"Add a distributed lock or in-process mutex. If the previous run is still active, skip the current tick and log a warning with the elapsed time of the stuck run."},{"scenario":"Service process crashes or OOMs","handling":"partial","recommendation":"Acceptable if running in a container orchestrator with restart policy, but add a liveness probe (the missing /health endpoint) so the orchestrator can detect a hung process, not just a crashed one."}],"retry_config":"None. Every transient failure (network blip, 503, DNS hiccup) is treated the same as a permanent failure. Must add retry with exponential backoff and jitter.","timeout_config":"None. The outbound HTTP call can hang indefinitely. Must add a 30-second connect + read timeout at minimum.","circuit_breakers":"None. If the upstream API is persistently down, the service will hammer it every 5 minutes with no backoff. Add a circuit breaker that opens after 5 consecutive failures and enters half-open state after 5 minutes.","graceful_degradation":"None. When sync fails, consumers of the local DB have no signal that stock counts are stale. Add a last_successful_sync_at timestamp (DB or metrics) so downstream systems can detect and react to stale data."},"operational_readiness":{"deployment_ready":false,"rollback_capable":true,"rollback_plan":"Re-deploy previous container image. No database migrations in this release, so rollback is clean. Verify stock counts were not corrupted by checking last_updated timestamps in the products table.","monitoring_coverage":"None. Zero metrics, no health endpoint, no alerting. The team will learn about failures only when a customer reports wrong stock counts.","on_call_documentation":"Missing. No runbook exists for: how to manually trigger a sync, how to verify data integrity after a failure, how to check the last successful sync time, or how to disable the sync in an emergency.","new_infrastructure_required":{"new_env_vars":["INVENTORY_API_TIMEOUT_MS — outbound call timeout","SYNC_RETRY_MAX_ATTEMPTS — retry ceiling","SYNC_RETRY_BASE_DELAY_MS — backoff base","CIRCUIT_BREAKER_THRESHOLD — failure count before open"],"new_services":[],"new_queues_topics":[],"config_changes":["Prometheus scrape target registration for the new /metrics endpoint","Alertmanager rules for sync_failures_total > 0 for 15m and last_successful_sync_age > 15m"],"infra_provisioning":["Health check endpoint registration in load balancer / container orchestrator liveness probe config"]},"database_safety":{"has_migrations":false,"reversible":true,"data_preserving":true,"large_table_impact":false,"details":"No schema migrations in this release. Risk is limited to stock count data correctness — a bad sync could write incorrect quantities but schema remains intact."},"verdict":"NOT READY"},"escalations":[{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No health endpoint for liveness/readiness probes","reason":"Container orchestrator cannot distinguish a hung process from a healthy one. A deadlocked sync loop will never be restarted. This is a hard prerequisite for any production Kubernetes/ECS deployment.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"missing_observability","issue":"Zero metrics and no structured logging","reason":"There is no way to detect, alert on, or diagnose sync failures in production. The on-call engineer has no dashboards, no alerts, and only two unstructured log lines to work with. We cannot assess operational health of something we cannot measure.","routed_to":"Coach K"},{"type":"infrastructure_gap","issue":"No timeout, retry, or circuit breaker on external API dependency","reason":"A single hung upstream connection can cascade into overlapping syncs, thread exhaustion, and stale inventory data with no automated recovery. These are not nice-to-haves — they are baseline resilience for any service calling an external API on a schedule.","routed_to":"Coach K"}],"confidence":{"level":90,"high_confidence_areas":["Resilience gaps are clear and unambiguous from the described architecture","Observability is objectively absent — zero metrics, minimal logging","Rollback path is clean given no migrations"],"low_confidence_areas":["Database transaction safety — unclear if the existing write path uses transactions","Overlap prevention — unclear if the scheduler has built-in mutual exclusion","Upstream API contract stability — no information on versioning or SLA"],"assumptions":["Service runs in a container orchestrator (Kubernetes or ECS) with restart policies","The external inventory API has no SLA guarantees provided to this team","The local database is relational and supports transactions","No other consumers write to the same stock count fields"]}}
